01_initial_request.input_data: Disulfide bonds (model compounds 1-4) and isocyanates (derived from carboxylic acids via Curtius rearrangement)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To investigate the nucleophilicity of disulfide bonds toward organic electrophiles via neighboring group participation and establish a new synthetic route for sulfur-containing compounds
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Synthesis yield of thiocarbamates (percentage) and orbital interaction energy (kcal mol⁻¹) from NBO analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Disulfide bonds are known to be cleaved by nucleophiles but little is known about their nucleophilicity toward organic electrophiles. [Gap] It is unknown if intermolecular reaction between disulfide and isocyanate can occur to form thiocarbamates. [Decision] To achieve investigation of nucleophilicity, chose to use Thiocarbamate synthesis to perform intermolecular reaction on Disulfide 1, expecting to obtain thiocarbamate.

parameters.conditions: 5 M HCl and dioxane, rt, overnight

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TLC analysis showed a long streak without distinct spots; methyl peak bonded to sulfur was not found in 1H NMR spectrum of the crude product.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Intermolecular reaction failed to show nucleophilic character of the disulfide bond. [Gap] It is unknown if flexible intramolecular reaction provides enough orbital overlap for nucleophilicity. [Decision] To achieve investigation of nucleophilicity, chose to use Thiocarbamate synthesis to perform flexible intramolecular reaction on Disulfide 2, expecting to obtain thiocarbamate.

parameters.conditions: N2, 65C, 4h; then 5 M HCl and dioxane, rt, overnight

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Major product (16 w/w%) isolated showing isocyanate peak in IR, but compound slowly decomposed and nucleophilic character not confirmed.

references.citation: 11 L. M. Miller, W.-J. Keune, D. Castagna, L. C. Young, E. L. Duffy, F. Potjewyd, F. Salgado-Polo, P. E. García, D. Semaan, J. M. Pritchard, A. Perrakis, S. J. F. Macdonald, C. Jamieson and A. J. B. Watson, J. Med. Chem., 2017, 60, 722–748.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Flexible intramolecular reaction did not confirm nucleophilicity. [Gap] It is hypothesized that rigid conformations are necessary for orbital overlap to enable nucleophilicity. [Decision] To achieve synthesis of thiocarbamates, chose to use Thiocarbamate synthesis to perform phenyl-enforced intramolecular reaction on Disulfide 3, expecting to obtain thiocarbamate.

parameters.conditions: N2, 65C, 4h; then 5 M HCl and dioxane, rt, overnight

references.citation: 12 F. Barthels, G. Marincola, T. Marciniak, M. Konhäuser, S. Hammerschmidt, J. Bierlmeier, U. Distler, P. R. Wich, S. Tenzer, D. Schwarzer, W. Ziebuhr and T. Schirmeister, ChemMedChem, 2020, 15, 839–850.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phenyl-enforced reaction was successful. [Gap] Need to verify if norbornyl-enforced rigid conformation also works for thiocarbamate formation. [Decision] To achieve synthesis of thiocarbamates, chose to use Thiocarbamate synthesis to perform norbornyl-enforced intramolecular reaction on Disulfide 4, expecting to obtain thiocarbamate.

parameters.conditions: N2, 65C, 4h; then 5 M HCl and dioxane, rt, overnight

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N was synthesized successfully. [Gap] The molecular structure needs to be unequivocally established to confirm the product identity. [Decision] To achieve structural validation, chose to use X-ray crystallographic structure study to perform structure determination on Thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N, expecting to obtain molecular structure.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Structure of thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N was unequivocally established.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental results suggest rigid conformations enable nucleophilicity. [Gap] Quantum level clarification of nucleophilicity is needed to understand orbital interactions. [Decision] To achieve clarification of nucleophilicity, chose to use Natural Bond Orbital (NBO) analysis to perform orbital interaction calculation on Isocyanate 3, expecting to obtain interaction energies.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Interaction of 1.96 kcal mol⁻¹ (toluene) and 5.18 kcal mol⁻¹ (water) between lone pair of S_ph and antibonding orbital of isocyanate.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] NBO analysis of Isocyanate 3 showed significant interactions. [Gap] Need to analyze Isocyanate 4 to compare orbital interactions in norbornyl system and explain its reactivity. [Decision] To achieve clarification of nucleophilicity, chose to use Natural Bond Orbital (NBO) analysis to perform orbital interaction calculation on Isocyanate 4, expecting to obtain interaction energies.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: No interaction in lowest-energy structures; Oxygen-protonated form showed interactions of 3.22 kcal mol⁻¹ and 1.69 kcal mol⁻¹.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: NBO analysis, X-ray crystallography, Yield measurement

synthesis_yield_3.interpretation: Yield of thiocarbamate 3-S(CO)N from phenyl-enforced intramolecular reaction

synthesis_yield_4.interpretation: Yield of thiocarbamate 4-S(CO)N from norbornyl-enforced intramolecular reaction

orbital_interaction_energy_3.interpretation: Interaction energy between lone pair of S_ph and antibonding orbital of isocyanate in water

orbital_interaction_energy_4.interpretation: Interaction energy between lone pair of S_nor and antibonding orbital of N=C in protonated form

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. Neighboring group participation by disulfide bonds was demonstrated, establishing a new synthetic route for cyclic thiocarbamates with quantifiable yields and orbital interaction energies.
